characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Freudian Trio: Dan is the Ego, Chris is the Id, Elise is the Superego. Nice, Mean, and In-Between: Chris is friendly and helpful, if a bit passive (nice), Dan is ill-tempered and rude to everyone around him and even wears a shirt labeled "Jerk" (mean), and Elise is theOnly Sane Womanwho can be just as dangerous and vengeful as Dan (in-between).

characters.name: Dan Mandel
characters.description: Accidental Murder: In "The Barber", he accidentally killed Mike due to pushing a barber making him drop his scissors on a bottle of hair spray. Ambiguously Bi: ...Or maybe just plain confused.One episode had him actually bring up "cooties" unironically, and this wasafteran entire episode where he had a girlfriend.He also calls Colby 'dreamy' and 'dreamboat' in "The Family Camping Trip". And claims he lost his fight with him because he gotlost in his captivating eyes. One episode had him actually bring up "cooties" unironically, and this wasafteran entire episode where he had a girlfriend. He also calls Colby 'dreamy' and 'dreamboat' in "The Family Camping Trip". And claims he lost his fight with him because he gotlost in his captivating eyes. Angry Fist-Shake: Whenever doing hisSkyward Scream, he furiously shakes is fist up in the air.

characters.name: Chris Pearson
characters.description: Anti-Villain: He's not evil, just too weak-willed to avoid getting roped into Dan'sZany Schemes. Author Avatar: Like Dan, he is based on andnamed afterone of the creators of the show, Chris Pearson. Beware the Nice Ones:In the climax of "Canada", Chris seems to actually be genuinely interested in helping Dan's plot to ice all of Canada in anger over the way his exiled Canadian ancestor was treated.In "The Lemonade Stand Gang", he puts his foot down at both Elise and Dan continuing to sink lower in their war against the Lemonade Stand Gang; he correctly realizes going lower will simply cause things to get worse. They need to go higher to win. His plan works and the Gang is busted by their parents; Dan actually compliments Chris afterwards.As shown in "The Telemarketer", asking Chris to make cocoa in the middle of the night, and then leaving before it's ready makes himextremely furious. This manages to intimidateDanof all people into joining in on his cocoa, implying that even a short-tempered jerk like him wouldn't want to upset Chris when it comes to cocoa.In "The Monster Under the Bed",it's revealed that the so-called "monster" was just a really elaborate"Scooby-Doo" Hoaxthat Chris made up with Elise's help, in order to ensure that Dan wouldn't interfere with their vacation plans. He even stated outright in his letter to Dan that this was because of too many "transgressions" from Dan. It turns out Chris can be just as cunning and vindictive as Dan after annoying him too much. In the climax of "Canada", Chris seems to actually be genuinely interested in helping Dan's plot to ice all of Canada in anger over the way his exiled Canadian ancestor was treated. In "The Lemonade Stand Gang", he puts his foot down at both Elise and Dan continuing to sink lower in their war against the Lemonade Stand Gang; he correctly realizes going lower will simply cause things to get worse. They need to go higher to win. His plan works and the Gang is busted by their parents; Dan actuall

characters.name: Elise Pearson
characters.description: Action Girlfriend: She is Chris's wife and a spy working for a secret organization, a fact that she tries to hide from her husband. Even as a housewife, she can put anyone down easily, which iswhyDan doesn't add her to his revenge list because he's too scared of her. In "The Telemarketer", he's shown erasing her from the list after seeing her beat a man senseless. Badass Bookworm: She's smart and knows how to kick some butt. Badass in Distress:"Technology": Where she's captured and brainwashed; she's rescued and has her mind restored by Dan"The Magician": This time rescued by Dan's proxy, an amateur magician.In "The Dinosaur"; this time, her brother is the one who saves her."The Common Cold"; Is captured andBound and Gaggedby a former supermodel turned supervillain. It's Chris who ends up saving her (and Dan) after he and Dan are captured, too. "Technology": Where she's captured and brainwashed; she's rescued and has her mind restored by Dan "The Magician": This time rescued by Dan's proxy, an amateur magician.

characters.name: Mr. Mumbles
characters.description: Amplified Animal Aptitude: Mr. Mumbles definitely knows what Dan's saying at times. Cats Are Mean: Averted. Mr. Mumbles is an affectionate and friendly cat. Generic Cuteness: Pretty much just looks like any other adorable cat. Morality Pet: Literally, for Dan. She is the only being in Dan's life that he shows consistent kindness to (even his best friend Chris gets vitriol and spite from Dan). Your Tomcat Is Pregnant: Dan names her Mr. Mumbles before he learns she's a girl.

characters.name: Crunchy
characters.description: A friendly and hapless hippie that shows up in various episodes. He is often an annoyance Dan. Butt-Monkey: He may be the one person in the show who suffers more than Dan does. In almost every single one of his appearances he ends up being beaten up, arrested, or just plain humilated. New-Age Retro Hippie: A classic example, he is a pacifist with long hair and loud clothing. New Job as the Plot Demands: He always has a different job whenever we see him. This includes the owner of an animal shelter, a sailor, and a back up juror. Nice Guy: He is annoying, but still a friendly guy.

characters.name: Hortence
characters.description: A girl who works at Burgerphile, she appears to know Dan and is one of the few people he is consistently nice and polite too. She went on strike after seeing Dan's protest (and briefly was his girlfriend) and later on becomes regional manager. She returns in another episode and seems to be the closest thing to a love interest for Dan. Burger Fool: Was an average employee at Bugerphile until she became promoted high enough for corporate. Early-Bird Cameo: Hortence makes a brief appearance in "Dan Vs. The Animal Shelter." before being a major character in "Dan Vs. Burgerphile.". Genius Ditz: May not be able to tell Latin from French, but she is a good customer representative and later a competent manager. Impossible Hourglass Figure: She has incredibly broad hips and a large bust that are attached by a long spindly waist. Interestingly this is more to emphasize her awkwardness and makes her look a bit lopsided, only looking more attractivewhen she gets into a dress.

characters.name: Wally Fitzpatrick
characters.description: Fan Disservice: In the scene where hisWolfmanalter-ego turns back to normal. Nice Guy: When not a werewolf, he is a pretty jolly and helpful guy. Wolf Man: Turns out to be the werewolf who scratched Dan's car.

characters.name: Elise's Parents
characters.description: Elise's parents, Don and Elise Sr., are pretty well-off and have a great disdain for Chris and to a lesser extent, Dan. Elise Sr. is just as badass as her daughter, but on the opposite side of the law. Action Mom:Elise Sr. in "Dan Vs. The Family Cruise". We see her fight her daughter, who is also the top operative in her own organization, to a stalemate when trying to steal for the Mafia. At Least I Admit It: Elise Sr. doesn't care for Chris or Dan, but she usually at least tries to be polite to their faces. Don is outwardly obnoxious and never tries to sugarcoat how he feels. Happily Married: They're both jerks, but they're completely devoted to each other. Jerkass: Even ignoring them beingObnoxious In-Lawsto Chris, Don's an abrasive, rude, jerk and Elise Sr. is manipulative and snobbish.

characters.name: Ninja Dave
characters.description: Last of His Kind: As mentioned Dave is the last of his ninja clan, essentially his family because he got head lice and missed the bus trip that killed them.

characters.name: Dr. Pullum
characters.description: Dan's dentist who was revealed to be a supervillain. Depraved Dentist: A dentist who also happens to be a super villain. Large Ham: Once he reveals himself as a supervillain, he just drops any sense of subtlety and startsdevouring the scenery. Mad Doctor: His absurdly complex plan is based around using dental work to brainwash people into doing his bidding.

characters.name: Barry Ditmer
characters.description: EccentricCEOof a major tech corporation with plans to take over the world. Big "NO!":Screams "NO!" when his security androids are destroyed. "Blackmail" Is Such an Ugly Word: When Elise reveals she knows about the mind control devices, Ditmer states that he prefers the term, "guiding synaptic processes from afar." Bourgeois Bohemian: He is similar to a lot of other hippies we see in the show, but a whole lot richer. Hates Being Touched: Ditmer refuses to shake hands, citing "human contact of any kind" as his primary phobia.

characters.name: Ilsa
characters.description: Barry Ditmer's robotic wife. Action Girl: While not quite on the level of Elise, Ilsa is able to put up a fight and even plant a mind control device on Elise. A.I.-cronym: Ilsa is an acronym for "Independent Logistics and Security Android." Robot Girl: Ilsa is a security droid that takes the form of a human woman. She speaks in a rootic cadence and has various technological accessories.

characters.name: Imposter Dan/The Imposter/The Telemarketer
characters.description: An enigmatic identity thief who steals the lives of misanthropes with no social lives. The Ace: He steals Dan's life and quickly becomes beloved by the neighbors, various charities, and even Chris and Elise. Affably Evil: He's a pretty friendly guy for an identity thief. Anti-Villain: He doesn't have anEvil Planor any sinister goal, all he wants is a good life, but his methods of getting it are a tad questionable. The Bad Guy Wins: He was successful in getting his revenge on Dan.

characters.name: Chef Puree
characters.description: The owner of Café Puree. Fat Bastard: He’s very rotund, and keeps his employees as slaves on top of being a jerk. French Jerk: He’s very rude and unethical and speaks in a French accent. Hate Sink: Someone who is surprising more unlikable thanDanof all people. His large Ego and treatment of his employees make it clear that he isn't meant to be liked. Jerkass: He’s rude, unethical, and keeps several chefs locked in a dungeon and forces them to cook food for Café Puree.

characters.name: Jeff
characters.description: The corrupt manager of Burgerphile, he cares more about his customer service record than actual service. Fears going back to Maryland. Anti-Villain: All he wants is to make sure he keeps his perfect customer service record so he doesn't have to go back to Maryland. Bad Boss: He's verbally abusive to his subordinates and tries to blackmail Hortense into getting rid Dan to leave by promising her a long-desired promotion. Berserk Button: Anything threatening his perfect record, namely Dan. Jerkass: In spades. He refuses to admit a simple mistake was made, insisting Dan was the one in the wrong and having him thrown out for complaining. When Dan chains himself up in the store to protest, Jeff goes to increasingly obnoxious and underhanded lengths to win out.

characters.name: The Hockey Masked Maniac
characters.description: Expy: OfJason Voorhees, being a chainsaw-wielding psychopath in ahockey maskout for blood. Serial Killer: He's basically a slasher villain who murdered multiple people in the woods about ten years ago. Strange Minds Think Alike: He wears a black t-shirt that says "Maniac" like Dan's "Jerk" shirt.

characters.name: Magnifico the Magnificent
characters.description: Jerkass: Stole from Chris (and various other people), humilated Dan and traumatized Elise when she was a child. No Celebrities Were Harmed: Resembles Criss Angel.

characters.name: Lemonade Stand Gang
characters.description: Kids Are Cruel: They use their lemonade stand to scam adults out of thousands of dollars and threaten anybody who tries to expose them.

characters.name: Honey O'Houlihan
characters.description: A femme fatale who seeks Dan's help about finding her cat, Poopsie. Femme Fatale: In true noir fashion, she tries to get what she wants by playing to Dan's sympathies (which works) and trying to wow him with her looks (which he misses most of the time). Harmless Villain: She's a pretty good manipulator, but she lacks the strength and resources to get her way. She needed a patsy (Dan) to get things done. When confronted over what she's done, she runs away and suffers a comical downfall.

characters.name: The Governor of California
characters.description: The Governor of California. He's clearly a little shady. Evil Brit:He turns out to be a British spy sent to make America stupider. Patriotic Fervor: His main defense against anyone who asks too many questions is that "unlikethem, he believes in this country". It's hard to believe it's how he really feels.And it certainly isn't.

characters.name: Amber
characters.description: Dan's anger management instructor. Ax-Crazy: She was chasing Chris with a chainsaw at the end of the episode, but she gets better. Berserk Button: As it turns out, littering is a big one for her. Just one random person dropping a piece of trash in front of her school immediately causes her to convulse with anger and struggle to to maintain control. Chris deliberately pushing this button prompts her to get a chainsaw and start chasing him around. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Downplayed. She starts out as a nice person, if having some anger issues beneath the surface. Upon getting a taste of revenge, she really likes it, so she goes after anyone that ever made her mad and threatens to have Dan sent to prison if he doesn't help. Blackmail: After finding out how much fun getting revenge is, she extorts Dan into following her orders. Dan can't really do anything because she can easily fail him and send him to prison.

characters.name: Terrifi-Guy
characters.description: An arrogant superhero who went after Dan after he destroyed Dan's car. Anti-Hero: He's destructive, rude, arrogant, selfish and all around just plain unlikable, yet he still does kind of save the day. Asshole Victim: It's kind of hard to feel bad for him after how he acted. Casting Gag: He's a Superman-like figure voiced by the guy behind theDC Animated Universe's Lex Luthor. Jerkass: Despite being a superhero, he's a very egotistical prick.

characters.name: Mr. Tedesco
characters.description: The camp counselor of Camp Atrocious who believes in brawn over brains and will torture kids to ensure it. Adults Are Useless: As the only authority figure at the camp, he rewards the bullies for picking on the weaker kids and mocks said weaker kids for complaining to him about it. Bilingual Bonus: "Tedesco" is Italian for "German". Brains Evil, Brawn Good: Mr. Tedesco's stated philosophy—he punishes Dan for gaining the spirit stick through cunning and subterfuge, rather than through brute force. The Bully: He's basically one grown up and given control of a summer camp.

characters.name: Helicopter Hal
characters.description: An egotistical local traffic-copter reporter and minor celebrity. Bunny-Ears Lawyer: Despite his massive ego and the attitude that comes with it, he is a fully qualified reporter and pilot. The Scapegoat: Dan needs to commandeer his helicopter for his actual plan, but he still punches Hal a couple times just because he's mad at traffic andbecause he can't digest butter. Hal rather sensibly points out that none of those things are his fault. Small Name, Big Ego: He thinks he’s way more famous than he actually is.
